title: VERVE Phase 1: Ownership & big picture
prompt: Tell me about a time you took ownership of a project that was failing or at risk. What was the broader context, what did you do, and what was the outcome?
hints: Use STAR: situation, your actions, measurable result.
Name what you owned vs what the team did.

title: VERVE Phase 2: Pushback on No
prompt: Describe a situation where you received a clear no from leadership on something you believed was important. How did you respond?
hints: Show evidence-based advocacy, not argument for its own sake.
Explain how you committed after the decision.

title: VERVE Phase 3: Force multiplier & mentorship
prompt: Tell me about a time you helped someone on your team level up — or unblocked others so the whole team shipped faster.
hints: Focus on artifacts left behind: docs, pairing, reviews.
Quantify team outcome, not solo heroics.

title: VERVE Phase 4: Pragmatism vs perfectionism
prompt: Give an example where you deliberately shipped something imperfect to meet a deadline or learn faster. What did you cut and what did you protect?
hints: Name trade-offs explicitly: safety, debt, scope.
Describe follow-up or monitoring after ship.

title: VERVE Phase 5: Candidate questions
prompt: We're at the end — what questions do you have for me about the team, role, or how we build software here?
hints: Ask about success at 30/90 days, code review culture, on-call.
Build on the interviewer's answers — show active listening.
